experiments.name: Purged/embargoed walk-forward retrain + stitched OOS portfolio as new promotion baseline
experiments.hypothesis: The headline Sharpe 2.13 was produced with a full-sample booster (meta_config.json n_folds=2, findings.jsonl caveat 'booster is full-sample — need WF-stitched portfolio for honest OOS'). If the meta-XGB is retrained per expanding fold with purging of trades whose 1H holding windows overlap the test fold and a >= max-holding-period embargo, and only out-of-fold gate decisions are stitched into one equity curve, the stitched Sharpe will be within 0.3 of 2.13 — i.e., the edge is real and not label-overlap leakage. If it falls further, current promotion decisions are unreliable and every downstream experiment is moot.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Build shared tooling (e.g. models/_shared/wf_harness.py): (1) expanding walk-forward folds over 2024-08..2026-07 (5-6 folds) plus a rolling 9-12mo variant; (2) purge training trades whose entry-to-exit window overlaps any test-fold trade, embargo 1-2 days of bars after each train window; (3) retrain the v15 meta-XGB per fold with threshold and any hyperparameters selected strictly on inner-fold data; (4) stitch fold-OOS trades into one portfolio equity curve and score full PASS_BAR on the stitch; (5) run the ANTI_OVERFIT frozen-rules holdout on the stitched preset. Optionally add CPCV paths to get a Sharpe distribution for confidence intervals. This becomes the mandatory harness every experiment below runs through.
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS meets full PASS_BAR (PF>1.2, |DD|<25%, Sharpe>0.5, n>=40, positive expectancy after costs) and stitched Sharpe >= 1.8 (within ~15% of the full-sample 2.13). If stitched Sharpe < 1.5, freeze all challenger promotion and treat v15's reported numbers as inflated.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: neutral — truth-revealing; establishes the honest baseline all other experiments must beat
lse_sources: models/_shared/findings.jsonl v15 caveat + SSRN CPCV literature entry

experiments.name: Lake A: soft 0.5x size-down on Coulling no-demand entry bars (no hard block)
experiments.hypothesis: v17b proved the no-demand signal carries quality information (PF 2.88, DD -9.6%) but hard-blocking destroyed capacity (98 trades) and Sharpe (1.82 vs 2.13). If all 130 v15 entries are kept and entries flagged no-demand (up bar + thin volume) are merely sized to 0.5x, PF and DD improve materially while trade count and therefore Sharpe capacity are preserved — capturing v17b's signal quality without its capacity tax.
experiments.implementation_sketch: In signal_engine.py sizing path: compute the existing Coulling no_demand flag at the entry bar; after the meta size_map output, multiply size by 0.5 when flagged, else 1.0. Zero changes to side rules, meta threshold, or feat_cols. Evaluate on the stitched WF harness (experiment 1) on 2024-08..2026-07, then the long 1D 2020-2026 stress. This is the repo's own written next_action in RESEARCH_NEXT.md Lake A with the pass bar already specified.
experiments.pass_criteria: On stitched OOS: Sharpe >= 2.03 (v15 - 0.1) AND PF >= 2.68 AND |DD| <= 13.2% AND trade count >= ~125 of 130 (no entries dropped) AND WR >= 62.3% not required but must not fall below 58%.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.0 to +0.1, with PF/DD improvement as the primary lift; best risk-adjusted first shot

experiments.name: Probability-calibrated continuous sizing replacing the binary 0.6 gate (Platt + fractional-Kelly p)
experiments.hypothesis: The hard thr=0.6 gate plus 3-step size_map (0.25/0.5/1.0 at 0.55/0.65) discards information: a 0.58 trade is all-or-nothing when it should be right-sized. If meta probabilities are Platt-calibrated on purged walk-forward OOF predictions (not isotonic — too flexible at n=130) and mapped continuously to size (p<0.50 skip, 0.50-0.65 scaled 0.5-1.0x, >0.65 full, optionally feeding the calibrated p into the half-Kelly formula floored/capped at [0.25x, 1.0x]), expectancy per unit of risk rises and P&L smooths, lifting Sharpe at roughly constant trade count.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Extend the meta stage: fit sklearn CalibratedClassifierCV-style sigmoid scaling per fold on OOF predictions from the WF harness; replace size_map with a continuous p->size function; variant B substitutes calibrated p for static historical WR in the existing half-Kelly sizing. Threshold/breakpoints selected on inner folds only (never test — logged failure). Evaluate on both the 1H stitched window and the long 1D window, where the coarse size_map was tuned on the short window (v16 findings' repeated next_action: threshold sweep on long 1D).
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe > 2.13 AND trades >= 120 AND PF >= 2.4 AND |DD| <= 13.2%; on the 1D 2020-2026 stress: Sharpe >= 0.85 (vs 0.75) with |DD| <= 22%. Calibration curve (reliability plot) must be monotone on OOF data.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.1 to +0.25; the most plausible direct route above 2.13

experiments.name: vol_z-tiered size multiplier (volume z-score concentrates capital, never gates)
experiments.hypothesis: Entry-bar volume z-score >= 1 is the repo's 'strongest OOS lift on stock trades' (BOOK_FINDINGS B5), and the earlier vol_z>=2 attempt failed only on thin training n (failure_class=needs_data), not absent OOS lift. If vol_z maps to a size multiplier (vol_z<0: 0.5x, 0-1: 1.0x, >=1: 1.25x capped by the Kelly cap) applied after the existing size_map, capital concentrates in volume-confirmed trades and expectancy and Sharpe rise with zero capacity loss.
experiments.implementation_sketch: In signal_engine.py: compute entry-bar volume z-score vs 20-bar rolling mean/std (shift-safe, entry bar close only); apply the 3-tier multiplier post-size_map. Do NOT add vol_z to feat_cols yet — RESEARCH_NEXT's Avoid list defers feat_cols retrains until a soft-gate variant beats v15. Tier breakpoints fixed a priori (0, 1), not swept. Evaluate on the WF-stitched harness; composable with v20 (multipliers multiply).
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe > 2.13 AND expectancy/trade > v15's AND trades unchanged (no entries dropped) AND |DD| <= 15%. Secondary: WR >= 62.3% on the vol_z>=1 subset confirms the B5 finding out-of-sample.

experiments.name: Correlation-aware concurrent-exposure scaling (soft size-down on correlated stacked longs)
experiments.hypothesis: TSLA/ARM/MU/IONQ/APLD are all high-beta tech; three concurrent longs are effectively one leveraged bet, and correlated simultaneous losers drive the -13.2% (and stress -22%) drawdowns. If a new gate-passing signal is sized by a soft multiplier (0.5-0.7x, scaled by 60-day rolling mean pairwise correlation to open positions when rho_bar > 0.6, with a crisis override assuming rho->1 when SPY realized vol is elevated), clustered-loss episodes shrink, portfolio vol falls at similar mean return, and Sharpe rises directly.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Portfolio-level wrapper in the backtest/live executor (no signal_engine.py side changes): track open positions; on new entry compute trailing 60d return correlation of candidate vs each open symbol; multiplier = 1.0 if rho_bar<=0.6 else linearly down to 0.5x at rho_bar>=0.85, additionally scaled by open-position count; cap total gross exposure when SPY 20d realized vol is in its top decile. Entries are never rejected. Parameters fixed a priori, not tuned.
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe >= 2.13 AND |DD| < 12% (must beat v15's -13.2%) AND trades unchanged AND PF >= 2.5; 1D 2020-2026 stress |DD| <= 18% (vs -22%). Also must not worsen Calmar vs v15.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.05 to +0.2, mostly via vol/DD reduction on the same return stream

experiments.name: Inverse-volatility per-symbol risk budgets under the existing Kelly/ATR overlay
experiments.hypothesis: Equal-capital sizing lets IONQ/APLD/TSLA dominate portfolio variance versus SPY/MU (the 60/40-is-90%-equity-risk problem). Setting base risk per trade proportional to 1/sigma_sym (trailing 60-day realized vol, normalized across the 6 symbols), layered under half-Kelly+ATR, equalizes risk contributions, smooths the equity curve, and lifts Sharpe without changing which trades fire. Parameter-light and side-neutral, so it composes with everything above.
experiments.implementation_sketch: In position sizing: base_risk_sym = account_risk * (1/sigma_sym) / sum_j(1/sigma_j), recomputed weekly from 60d realized vol; no leverage on low-vol sleeves (cap all weights at 1.0x of current per-trade risk — only high-vol sleeves shrink). Everything else unchanged. Evaluate standalone on the WF harness first, then combined with the best of v20-v23 (watch for over-stacking: v16 showed overlay stacking can shave Sharpe, so combined variants must each re-clear the bar).
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe > 2.13 AND |DD| <= 13.2% AND trades unchanged AND PF >= 2.5; if Sharpe lands in [2.03, 2.13] but DD improves >2pp, log as documented lower-DD sleeve (v16 precedent) and do not promote.

experiments.name: Meta feature-matrix hygiene: stationarity (ADF) + correlation pruning + fold-stable SHAP + small-sample XGB regularization
experiments.hypothesis: With only 130 labeled trades the meta-XGB is data-starved and almost certainly over-parameterized. A one-time audit — (a) ADF-test every feat_col and replace level-like features (dollar distances to POC/VAH/VAL, raw volume) with ATR/percent-normalized forms, (b) drop feature pairs with |corr|>0.9, (c) drop features with near-zero or fold-unstable SHAP/gain importance across WF folds, (d) point-in-time/shift(1) audit, (e) re-tune to a small-sample config (max_depth 2-3, learning_rate 0.01-0.05, subsample 0.8, raised min_child_weight, early stopping on the last time-ordered fold only) — reduces gate variance and closes part of the gap between the 2yr Sharpe 2.13 and the 1D stress Sharpe 0.75, where price-level nonstationarity bites hardest.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Script in models/_shared/research/: load v15 feat matrix, run statsmodels ADF per feature over 2020-2026, correlation matrix, per-fold SHAP via the WF harness; emit a pruned feat_cols list + normalized replacements; retrain with the regularized config inside the WF harness. All selection happens inside training folds. Primary side rules untouched.
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe >= 2.13 (must not degrade) AND 1D 2020-2026 stress Sharpe >= 0.85 (vs 0.75) AND cross-fold gate-decision agreement (fraction of trades gated identically across adjacent folds) improves vs the unpruned model; PF >= 2.5, trades >= 120.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: roughly flat on 1H window, +0.1 to +0.2 on the long stress window; main payoff is holdout robustness

experiments.name: Portfolio volatility targeting on the fixed v15 trade stream (CML scaling, optional GARCH sigma forecast)
experiments.hypothesis: v15's returns cluster by vol regime, and its worst DD stretches coincide with realized-vol spikes (1D stress DD -22%). Treating the v15 stream as a fixed tangency portfolio and scaling gross exposure weekly by target_vol / trailing_20d_realized_vol of the strategy equity curve (capped at 1.0x — no leverage; take min with the half-Kelly scaler) deleverages exactly when vol and DD spike, raising Sharpe through a lower vol denominator. Variant B replaces trailing realized vol with a per-symbol GARCH(1,1) 1-5d sigma forecast for soft 0.5x size-down when forecast vol is top-quintile.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Equity-curve-level scaler in the backtest runner: weekly, scale = min(1.0, target_vol/realized_20d_vol), applied to all new entry sizes; composition, entries, sides, gate untouched. GARCH variant: arch package, walk-forward refit monthly per symbol, feed only the sizing layer (never a gate — hard vol gates cost Sharpe 2.13->1.75). Target_vol set to v15's full-period realized vol, not tuned. v16's lesson applies: this is an overlay, so it must beat v15 Sharpe outright or it gets logged, not promoted.
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe > 2.13 AND trades unchanged; 1D 2020-2026 stress: Sharpe >= 0.9 AND |DD| <= 17% (vs 0.75/-22%). If short-window Sharpe in [2.03,2.13] but stress metrics clear, log as stress-hardened sleeve only.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.0 to +0.1 short window, +0.15 to +0.3 on the long stress window

experiments.name: Regime feature pack for the meta-XGB (QQQ trend, Mag7 breadth, vol-regime bucket, rolling beta) — GATED behind a soft-gate win
experiments.hypothesis: v15's feat_cols contain only spy_htf_green as regime context, yet per-symbol data shows regime is the largest un-modeled conditional (APLD 88.9% WR under f_qqq_trend vs ARM 44.4%). Adding a small, pre-audited pack — QQQ trend state, Mag7-breadth^QQQ, realized-vol-regime bucket, 60-90 bar rolling beta/R² vs SPY — as meta features (WHETHER/HOW MUCH inputs only, never side) lets the gate learn regime-conditional odds, raising filtered WR above 62.3% and PF without hard-gating capacity. Per RESEARCH_NEXT's Avoid list, this feat_cols retrain runs ONLY after one of v20-v22 beats v15 on stitched OOS Sharpe; it also completes the pending official v18_dual_sleeve harness run (equity/DD) as its precursor evidence.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Step 1: finish the pending official harness backtest of v18_dual_sleeve with Mag7 data and score PASS_BAR (repo-scaffolded, cheap). Step 2: compute the 4-6 regime features point-in-time daily; add to feat_cols; retrain inside the WF harness with the hygiene-pruned config; adjudicate each new feature by fold-stable SHAP before keeping (drop any with |corr|>0.9 to existing features). Kalman-slope or K-means-regime-ID variants are second-line substitutes only if the direct features show unstable importance.
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS Sharpe > best-prior-challenger AND > 2.13 AND WR >= 62.3% AND trades >= 110 AND PF >= 2.68; every retained feature shows same-sign SHAP direction in >= 4 of 5 folds. Sequencing precondition: a v20/v21/v22 variant already beat v15 Sharpe on the stitch.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.1 to +0.2 with WR lift, conditional on the sequencing gate

experiments.name: Promotion-report hardening: Calmar, average DD, underwater duration + crisis-correlation stress scenario
experiments.hypothesis: Max DD and Sharpe alone let a challenger win via a lucky window while sitting underwater longer or depending on benign-period diversification. Adding Calmar, average drawdown, max underwater duration, and a crisis scenario (recompute DD/Sharpe assuming all concurrent positions perfectly correlated — sum absolute exposures) to the OOS reporter, and requiring challengers not to worsen Calmar or underwater duration vs v15, prevents false promotions at near-zero cost and zero trading-rule changes.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Extend the backtest reporter used by the WF harness: compute Calmar (ann. return / MDD), mean DD, max underwater bars, plus a crisis-corr rerun of the stitched curve; write all into results.json and findings.jsonl entries. Encode as promotion rules alongside PASS_BAR. One afternoon of work; every experiment above then consumes it for free.
experiments.pass_criteria: Not a Sharpe experiment — pass = metrics implemented, v15 baseline values recorded (Calmar, underwater duration on 2024-08..2026-07 and the 1D stress), crisis-scenario DD for v15 documented and < 25%; all subsequent promotions require Calmar >= v15's and crisis-DD < 25%.
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: neutral — prevents accepting fake Sharpe improvements

experiments.name: Lake B: 4H/1D climax / effort-vs-result as soft exit tightener (entries untouched)
experiments.hypothesis: The full climax/effort stack over-filtered on 1H (v17 FAIL, wrong_hypothesis), but the logged next_action is to retest on coarser grain as an exit modifier. If buying-climax / effort-vs-result flags computed on 4H/1D bars tighten the trail or halve remaining size on OPEN positions only, the loser tail from blow-off bars shrinks with zero capacity cost, improving PF and DD while entry count and WR stay fixed.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Reuse the existing v17 Coulling detectors resampled to 4H/1D; in the position-management loop, when the higher TF flags climax while a position is open: switch trail from ATR-k to ATR-k/2 (or exit half). No entry logic touched. Audit output must show the modified exits disproportionately hit trades that were losers under v15 ('fewer climax-chase losers' per RESEARCH_NEXT Lake B).
experiments.pass_criteria: Stitched OOS: entry count identical to v15, Sharpe >= 2.03, PF >= 2.68, |DD| < 13.2%; trade audit shows net R improvement concentrated in the bottom-decile v15 trades. If PF/DD improve but Sharpe < 2.03, do not promote (v17b lesson).
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.0 to +0.1, primarily PF/DD via cheaper exits

experiments.name: Small-cap peer screen to grow the 83%-WR sniper sleeve toward n>=40 (satellite only) + one low-correlation diversifier candidate
experiments.hypothesis: The APLD+IONQ sniper sleeve (WR 83.3%, PF 19.7) fails PASS_BAR only on n=12; the statistically valid fix is more names sharing the DNA, not more filters. Screening for APLD-like microstructure (daily vol 2.5-3%, $60-120M dollar volume, high volume CV, sparse options chains, low institutional footprint), verifying the same DNA lifts (VWAP/vol_expand/red_flag) per candidate on train data before admission, and running 3-6 admitted peers through the unchanged pipeline grows sleeve n past 40 and raises blended-book WR — the direct route to 'keep or raise 62.3%' without touching v15. Secondarily, screen 1-2 genuinely low-correlation liquid names (non-tech) for a capped sleeve under the same locked rules, admitted only on sleeve-level PASS_BAR.
experiments.implementation_sketch: Script over the existing OHLCV + FEATURE_INSIGHTS pipeline: universe scan -> microstructure filter -> per-name DNA-lift verification (Wilson intervals given small n) -> admit -> backtest expanded sleeve 2024-2026 through the WF harness. Book composition stays wA*Sleeve + wB*v15 with a fixed satellite cap mirroring the current sniper weighting; never replaces v15.
experiments.pass_criteria: Expanded sleeve: n >= 40 OOS, WR >= 75%, PF > 1.2 (full PASS_BAR); blended book: WR >= 62.3% AND Sharpe >= 2.13 AND |DD| <= 13.2%. Any new diversifier symbol admits only if its standalone stitched sleeve passes PF>1.2 at n>=40. No new filters may be added to force WR (logged trap).
experiments.expected_sharpe_direction: +0.05 to +0.15 blended, via more independent bets and higher blended WR; highest effort in the list (data acquisition + screening)

discarded: ARIMA short-term mean forecasting as a signal — price-predicting model as signal source violates the locked architecture and rhymes with the failed naive-XGB-next-close; its useful half (GARCH vol) is kept in v25_vol_target.
LSTM/Transformer sequence models — n=130 tabular trade outcomes is orders of magnitude below what sequence models need; the source page itself disqualifies the use case.
Neural networks for trade-outcome prediction — same n<1K tabular disqualification per the source's own guidance; XGBoost stays.
Regression models forecasting next price/return — explicitly banned as primary by the architecture lock and the logged poc_va_xgb failure.
Full Markowitz mean-variance weight optimization — per-symbol expected returns from 130 trades are noise; kept only as its estimation-free degenerate form (inverse-vol budgets, v24).
Time-series stacking with a diverse base model — high overfit risk at n=130 for a +1-5% expected squeeze per the source itself; revisit only after sample size grows.
PCA of meta features — second-line hygiene made redundant by correlation pruning in meta_feature_hygiene; XGB tolerates residual correlation.
Cross-sectional momentum rank — degenerate with a 6-symbol universe (rank in sixths); near-zero power for the added feature-count cost.
IV-minus-realized-vol spread meta feature — GEX chain snapshots lack point-in-time IV history for the backtest window; deferred to live-forward evaluation only, not a rankable experiment now.

Isolation Forest / one-class SVM anomaly size-down — redundant with the vol-targeting + GARCH + correlation-throttle stack, which covers the same 'abnormal state -> smaller size' mechanism with fewer fitted parameters.

Historical-simulation ES/CVaR exposure budget — tail estimate too noisy at ~130 trades of P&L history to be a fitted control; crisis-correlation stress scenario (holdout_report_hardening) covers the tail-risk validation need cheaply.

Drawdown-triggered soft equity throttle — plausible but overlaps vol targeting (both deleverage in bad stretches) and v16 already showed overlay stacking shaves Sharpe; hold as a fallback if v25 fails its bar.

Bucket-specific FEATURE_INSIGHTS rebuild for large caps — high overfit risk (per-flag lift on small per-symbol n) and sequenced behind v18_dual_sleeve official run inside v26; not a standalone experiment yet.
Kalman filter and K-means regime models as standalone experiments — folded into v26_regime_meta_features as second-line variants; running them separately would be 3 variants of one idea.

All TRAP entries (hard entry gates, WR>=90% chasing, reactive node+MA-cloud primary, random k-fold, test-set threshold tuning, premature feat_cols retrains) — retained as constraints baked into every experiment's design and sequencing, not as experiments.
